description: Independent blogs popular on Hacker News (opinion leader layer)

trends.description: AI is increasingly integrated into the software development lifecycle, from code generation and compilation to testing and optimization. This trend suggests a future where AI tools augment and potentially transform how software is created, impacting developer productivity and the nature of programming tasks themselves.
evidence: Ladybird adopts Rust, with help from AI
The Claude C Compiler: What It Reveals About the Future of Software

trends.description: There's a strong focus on improving the performance and scalability of AI models, especially large language models (LLMs). This includes optimizing inference speed, exploring local AI solutions, and benchmarking different models. The goal is to make AI more accessible and efficient for a wider range of applications.
evidence: ggml.ai joins Hugging Face to ensure the long-term progress of Local AI
Frigate with Hailo for object detection on a Raspberry Pi

trends.description: A counter-narrative is emerging that questions the overblown hype surrounding AI, particularly AGI and generative AI. This trend highlights the limitations and potential scams associated with current AI technologies, urging a more critical and realistic assessment of their capabilities and impact.
evidence: Rumors of AGI’s arrival have been greatly exaggerated
Turns out Generative AI was a scam
Premium: The Hater's Guide to Anthropic

posts.title: Writing about Agentic Engineering Patterns
posts.author: Simon Willison
posts.summary:
I've started a new project to collect and document Agentic Engineering Patterns - coding practices and patterns to help get the best results out of this new era of coding agent development we find ourselves entering.

I'm using Agentic Engineering to refer to building software using coding agents - tools like Claude Code and OpenAI Codex, where the defining feature is that they
posts.llm_summary: A new project documents Agentic Engineering Patterns, highlighting coding practices for AI agents.

posts.title: Rumors of AGI’s arrival have been greatly exaggerated
posts.author: Gary Marcus
posts.summary: Statistical approximation ≠ general intelligence
posts.llm_summary: Contends that current AI systems are merely statistical approximations and fall far short of achieving Artificial General Intelligence (AGI).

posts.title: Ladybird adopts Rust, with help from AI
posts.author: Simon Willison
posts.summary:
Ladybird adopts Rust, with help from AI

Really interesting case-study from Andreas Kling on advanced, sophisticated use of coding agents for ambitious coding projects with critical code. After a few years hoping Swift's platform support outside of the Apple ecosystem would mature they switched tracks to Rust their memory-safe language of choice, starting with an AI-assisted port of a critical library:
posts.llm_summary: Ladybird browser adopts Rust with AI assistance, showcasing advanced AI usage in software development.

posts.llm_summary: Taalas serves Llama 3.1 8B at 17,000 tokens/second, showcasing new hardware for AI acceleration.

posts.llm_summary: Google's Gemini 3.1 Pro is a new large language model that is priced competitively.

posts.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
posts.author: BrianKrebs
posts.summary: Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic
posts.llm_summary: Details a sophisticated phishing service that proxies real login pages and MFA, posing a significant security threat.

Rather than b…

posts.title: Writing code is cheap now
posts.author: Simon Willison
posts.summary:
Agentic Engineering Patterns >

The biggest challenge in adopting agentic engineering practices is getting comfortable with the consequences of the fact that writing code is cheap now.

Code has always been expensive. Producing a few hundred lines of clean, tested code takes most software developers a full day or more. Many of our engineering habits, at both the macro and micro le
posts.llm_summary: The challenge in adopting agentic engineering is shifting focus from code writing to higher-level concerns.

posts.llm_summary: ggml.ai joins Hugging Face to advance the development of Local AI technologies.

Long running agentic products like Claude Code are made feasible by prompt caching which allows us to reuse computation from previous roundtrips and significantly decrease latency and cost. [...]

posts.llm_summary: Prompt caching is enabling long-running agentic AI products like Claude Code by reusing computation.

posts.llm_summary: Using Frigate with Hailo for object detection on a Raspberry Pi improves security camera performance.

posts.title: Red/green TDD
posts.author: Simon Willison
posts.summary:
Agentic Engineering Patterns >

"Use red/green TDD" is a pleasingly succinct way to get better results out of a coding agent.

TDD stands for Test Driven Development. It's a programming style where you ensure every piece of code you write is accompanied by automated tests that demonstrate the code works.

The most disciplined form of TDD is test-first development. Yo
posts.llm_summary: Advocating for red/green TDD as a useful practice within agentic engineering workflows.

posts.title: What's so hard about continuous learning?
posts.author: Sean Goedecke
posts.summary:
Why can’t models continue to get smarter after they’re deployed? If you hire a human employee, they will grow more familiar with your systems over time, and (if they stick around long enough) eventually become a genuine domain expert. AI models are not like this. They are always exactly as capable as the first moment you use them.

This is because model weights are frozen once the model is released. The model can only “learn” as much as can be stuffed into its context window: in effect,
posts.llm_summary: An exploration of the challenges of continuous learning for AI models after deployment.

posts.title: Recovering lost code
posts.author: Simon Willison
posts.summary:

... found it! Turns out I'd been hacking on a random prototype in /tmp and then my computer crashed and rebooted and I lost the code... but it's all still there in ~/.claude/projects/ session logs and Claude Code can extract it out and spin up the missing feature aga

posts.title: Insider amnesia
posts.author: Sean Goedecke
posts.summary:
Speculation about what’s really going on inside a tech company is almost always wrong.

When some problem with your company is posted on the internet, and you read people’s thoughts on it, their thoughts are almost always ridiculous. For instance, they might blame product managers for a particular decision, when in fact the decision in question was engineering-driven and the product org was pushing back on it. Or they might attribute an incident to overuse of AI, when the system in que
posts.llm_summary: Speculation about what's happening inside a tech company is usually wrong.

Apple Support:

If iPhone isn’t responding, and you can’t turn it off then on, try forcing it to restart.

  1. Press and quickly release the volume up button.
  2. Press and quickly release the volume down button.
  3. Press and hold the side button.
  4. When the Apple logo appears, release the side button.

posts.title: forecourt networking

posts.summary:
The way I see it, few parts of American life are as quintessentially American as buying gas. We love our cars, we love our oil, and an industry about as old as automobiles themselves has developed a highly consistent, fully automated, and fairly user friendly system for filling the former with the latter.

I grew up in Oregon. While these rules have since been relaxed, many know Oregon for its long identity as one of two states where you cannot pump your own gas (the other being New Jer
posts.llm_summary: Explores the networking aspects of gas stations in American culture.

posts.title: the essence of frigidity
posts.author: computer.rip (J. B. Crawford)
posts.summary: The front of the American grocery store contains a strange, liminal space: the transitional area between parking lot and checkstand, along the front exterior and interior of the building, that fills with oddball commodities. Ice is a fixture at nearly every store, filtered water at most, firewood at some. This retail purgatory, both too early and too late in the shopping journey for impulse purchases, is mostly good only for items people know they will need as they check out. One of the stand
posts.llm_summary: Analyzes the transitional space at the front of American grocery stores.

We think that we're converting time into energy... that's the engineering principle.

In the 1820s, stacks, ovens, and gasometers rose over the docklands of Dublin. The Hibernian Gas Company, one of several gasworks that would eventually occupy the land around the Grand Canal Docks, heated coal to produce town gas. That gas would soon supply thousands of lights on the streets of Dublin, a quiet revolution in municipal development that paved the way for electri
